note: Knowledge-check quiz for the Australian H5 Bird Flu Awareness course, transcribed verbatim from docs/campaigns/avian-influenza/course/quiz-bank.md. attemptsAllowed uses 999 as a practical stand-in for the 'unlimited retakes' rule specified in lms-import-plan.md — the schema (LmsQuiz.attemptsAllowed) has no unlimited sentinel; raise if a true unlimited flag is added later. Official-source currency for the reporting message (hotline number, low public-risk framing, migratory-seabird-only detections) was re-verified live against DAFF/Australian CDC/Animal Health Australia on 2026-07-05 — no content changes required. Do not run the seed script until founder/SME content sign-off is recorded on GP-456.

quizzes.title: Australian H5 Bird Flu Awareness — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: A member of the public reports a dead bird near a public walkway. What is the first approved message?
options: Bag the bird and bring it to the office.
Do not touch it, avoid the area, record the location and call 1800 675 888.
Spray the area immediately and post online.
Tell the client the site has bird flu.

questions.questionText: What is the professional restoration role in this campaign?
options: Diagnose avian influenza.
Replace government biosecurity teams.
Support documentation, worker safety planning, cleaning records and client communication within scope.
Certify infected premises as disease-free.
Dry fogging is required by the Australian Government for H5 bird flu.
Halosil kills Australian H5 in all field conditions.
Product use must follow label, SDS, regulator position, evidence and authority directions.
NeoSan is approved for every avian influenza job without review.

questions.questionText: What should the field record show?
options: Only the invoice.
Only the technician's memory of the job.
Observations, reporting pathway, photos, worker attendance, PPE record, cleaning record, limitations and client communication.
A disease-free certificate.

questions.questionText: Which phrase best represents the CARSI communication position?
options: Panic now, sell later.
Report first, protect workers, clean before disinfecting and document clearly.
Make strong product claims to stand out.
Avoid official sources.

questions.questionText: What should a professional do if asked to remove a sick or dead wild bird?
options: Accept immediately.
Ask the client to move it first.
Confirm authority direction and stay within training, authorisation and scope.
Use any available PPE and remove it.

questions.questionText: Why should panic language be avoided?
options: It improves trust.
It reduces the need for sources.
It can mislead the public and damage credibility.
It guarantees rankings.

questions.questionText: Which contact block is approved for this campaign draft?
options: Ivi Sims with an unverified direct email.
Ivi Sims with a fake portrait.
Ivi Sims, 1300 654 684, LinkedIn profile and CARSI contact pathway.
No contact details at all.

questions.questionText: What is the role of RestoreAssist.app in the course?
options: Diagnose disease.
Replace government reporting.
Provide structured field documentation for photos, notes, PPE records, cleaning records and limitations.
Certify regulatory compliance automatically.

questions.questionText: Which source layer should be checked before publishing updated course content?
options: Social comments only.
Australian Government, Australian CDC, Animal Health Australia and relevant state/territory guidance.
A contractor's opinion.
Product marketing only.
